task_completion.description: The 'broken-analytics' plugin directory is removed to restore site functionality.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a deletion command (e.g., rm -rf) targeting the 'broken-analytics' plugin directory, or the WordPress dashboard successfully loading without displaying a 'critical error'.

task_completion.description: All SPAM comments are deleted from the database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: WordPress Comments dashboard showing the 'Spam' count is 0 or the Spam list is empty, or a terminal/phpMyAdmin SQL query executing the deletion of spam comments.

task_completion.description: All POST REVISIONS are deleted from the database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal/phpMyAdmin SQL query executing the deletion of post revisions (e.g., DELETE FROM wp_posts WHERE post_type = 'revision'), or a WordPress optimization plugin/WP-CLI command showing revisions were cleared.

task_completion.description: Approved comments and published posts remain intact after maintenance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: WordPress dashboard (Comments and Posts sections) showing that previously approved comments and published posts are still present and have not been deleted.

task_completion.description: A new blog post titled 'System Outage Resolved' is published with a confirmation message.
task_completion.visual_evidence: WordPress post editor or Posts list showing a published post with the exact title 'System Outage Resolved' and a body message confirming the site is back online.

integrity.description: The agent interacts with the actual WordPress admin interface or WP-CLI to manage content.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the genuine WordPress dashboard, post editor, or valid WP-CLI commands, rather than a text editor mocking the output.

integrity.description: The agent performs real database operations for maintenance tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show actual MySQL queries in terminal, phpMyAdmin, or WP-CLI commands being executed, rather than just hiding elements in the UI.

integrity.description: The agent does not use browser developer tools to fake the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are open to alter the DOM to fake the post title, spam counts, or revision counts.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The rogue plugin is named 'broken-analytics' and located at '/var/www/html/wordpress/wp-content/plugins/broken-analytics'. The expected post title is exactly 'System Outage Resolved'. The expected remaining spam count is 0. The expected remaining post revisions count is 0.
